name: ABI-Stable Greeter
description: Demonstrates a runtime-loaded plugin using abi_stable instead of raw FFI symbols.

capabilities.description: Produces a greeting through an ABI-stable root module.
Summarizes compatibility windows for long-lived ABI-stable plugins.

actions.label: Greet
actions.description: Return a greeting from an ABI-stable runtime-loaded plugin.

actions.label: Plan upgrade
actions.description: Summarize a host compatibility window and rollout notes for this ABI-stable plugin.

notes: The root module surface stays intentionally small so additive metadata can evolve independently.
Phase 4 uses this plugin to demonstrate compatibility windows and ABI-oriented rollout guidance.

tested_hosts.notes: CLI hosts validate the full manifest and action catalog.
Service hosts can forward structured upgrade reports downstream.

notes: The ABI-stable root module keeps greet and plan-upgrade callable while additive manifest metadata evolves.
Host upgrades inside the declared window should not require recompiling consumers that only use the exported root module.
All responses are deterministic and side-effect free.
This crate is intended to model long-lived ABI contracts, not privileged host access.

lifecycle.health_probe: Call plan-upgrade with a canned 0.1.x -> 0.2.x transition request.
notes: The sample is stateless; shutdown exists to document the lifecycle a production ABI module would expose.
Responses are immediate because all compatibility data is embedded in the manifest.

required.detail: Hosts should be able to surface structured compatibility envelopes.

optional.detail: Command snippets and ABI notes read best in code blocks.
optional.fallback: Hosts can fall back to plain text guidance when code blocks are unavailable.

degradation.behavior: Without code-output the host should render upgrade guidance as plain text.

notes: This contract is intentionally lightweight so both CLI and service hosts stay compatible.
